evidence: We are integrating artificial intelligence and machine learning technologies ('AI') into our business operations, products and services, while continuing to explore the opportunities that AI could bring to the company.
We may be unable to devote adequate financial resources to develop or acquire new AI technologies and systems in the future.

Advancements in our offering of software and service solutions will help our customers more efficiently operate a digitally connected smart lab that can derive value out of data analytics, artificial intelligence and robotics.
We are integrating artificial intelligence and machine learning technologies ('AI') into our business operations, products and services.

AI technologies may be developed using inaccurate, incomplete, flawed or biased algorithms, training methodologies or data, which could result in competitive harm, regulatory penalties, legal liability, or brand or reputational harm.
The input of confidential information or trade secrets into AI systems may result in the loss of intellectual property, proprietary rights, or attorney-client privilege.

AI is subject to a dynamic and rapidly evolving legal and regulatory environment, which, without appropriate review, governance and risk management, could expose the company to unforeseen legal or regulatory scrutiny and liabilities.

limitations: No quantified AI capex, headcount, or budget figures appear in the excerpts; investment intensity scored 2 based on generic integration language without named systems, partners, or amounts.
AI narrative concreteness scored 3: 'smart lab' and integration references name a use-case direction but no deployment status or metrics are disclosed.

Risk disclosure scored 4 rather than 5: multiple company-specific risk mechanisms are described (IP loss, regulatory exposure, data leakage, biased outputs) with named pathways to harm, but no quantified risk figures or named governance/oversight bodies appear in the excerpts.
